sourceRef: tip:Documenting exceptions so future you trusts them

slides.headline: Document exceptions now
slides.body: Ensure future compliance and save time

slides.headline: Use the COE
slides.body: Start with the Certificate of Eligibility (COE) form. It lists all exceptions.

slides.headline: Review 22-1999
slides.body: Check VA Form 22-1999 for specific exceptions and documentation.

slides.headline: Use 85/15 rule
slides.body: Apply the 85/15 rule to determine which exceptions require documentation.

slides.headline: Document clearly
slides.body: Be specific and clear in your documentation to avoid future confusion.

slides.headline: Save time
slides.body: By documenting exceptions, you can quickly verify compliance later.
Learn more about documenting exceptions and saving time. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Document exceptions now to save time and ensure future compliance. Follow these steps: 1. Use the COE, 2. Review 22-1999, 3. Apply the 85/15 rule, 4. Document clearly, 5. Save time. Learn more at aimilitaryservices.com.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 4 of 5, which could benefit from a more compelling headline to draw attention.
